title: ammophila brevipilis
intro: Ammophila brevipilis, also known as the dwarf sandwort, is a low-growing, succulent plant native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ammophila brevipilis include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Ammophila brevipilis are typically made into infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
